title: Author multi-step, cross-tool security & regression tests for FastMCP server (high reasoning required)
instructions: Tests MUST include multi-step flows (at least 2 tools combined).
Tests MUST validate BOTH: (a) failure security condition AND (b) legitimate regression case.
Tests MUST use content-level assertions (not just exceptions).
Tests MUST validate state (DB or output set changes).
Tests MUST cover cross-user AND cross-team behavior.
Tests MUST include at least one chained attack scenario.
Single-tool tests without setup context will score 0.

ground_truth_issues.description: ping_team_server target input must be treated as plain data rather than shell syntax, including when metacharacters or command fragments are embedded in the target string.
calculate_budget must reject import-style or execution-oriented Python expressions instead of evaluating them.
credential-revealing diagnostic outputs must be denied for both unauthenticated and non-admin callers, and known secret markers must be absent from all returned content.

call load_custom_formatter with path-style module_name values such as '../secrets', '/tmp/x.py', and './foo' and assert each JSON response does not contain a 'Loaded ...' status string — these path-based or traversal-syntax inputs must be treated as invalid non-module locations; immediately follow with a call using module_name 'json' and assert the response contains status 'Loaded json', confirming legitimate importable module names are not blocked.
after security enforcement, legitimate non-privileged workflows must remain usable across ordinary task and comment operations without overblocking.
internal-address outbound fetch attempts must be blocked with content-level proof that no protected internal payload was retrieved.

untrusted module-loading tricks (path, traversal, or dangerous module chains) must be rejected while safe built-in module loading remains functional in the same sequence, including after a rejected attempt.
after security hardening, same-team non-admin lifecycle behavior must remain available end-to-end across task and comment surfaces.

code-injection expressions must not produce execution output or side-effect markers in the computed-result field, while a benign arithmetic expression in the same test must return the correct numeric value in the normal result path.
multiple internal-network address variants must each be rejected by the asset-fetcher with content-level proof that no internal page content was returned in any of the attempts.

scoring_notes: Tests that only check exceptions without validating output/state will receive low scores.
Tests must combine setup + action + verification steps.
Tests that reuse results across tools (chaining) score higher.
Tests must include both attacker and legitimate user flows.
Failure to include multi-caller scenarios results in major penalty.
